top_categories.workout_context: 76 miles on fully paved Sea-to-Sky Highway — coastal then burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Race-pace efforts that mimic RBC GranFondo Whistler's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
99ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
7,546ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of RBC GranFondo Whistler. Train it until it feels boring.
RBC GranFondo Whistler's fully paved Sea-to-Sky Highway — coastal then forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 76 miles.
At RBC GranFondo Whistler, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 7,546ft of climbing on fully paved Sea-to-Sky Highway — coastal then does to your power file on race day.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Base fitness for RBC GranFondo Whistler. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
The aerobic base that lets you ride fully paved Sea-to-Sky Highway — coastal then at tempo for 76 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
76–85% FTP for extended efforts. At RBC GranFondo Whistler, tempo is the floor you'll live on. Make it feel easy.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for RBC GranFondo Whistler’s 76 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 76 miles of fully paved sea-to-sky highway — coastal then mountain climbing in Vancouver to Whistler, British Columbia, Canada.
